flow: cva6 veri-testharness (work-ver/Variane_testharness); Embench-IoT v1.0 workloads compiled against the CVA6 bare-metal BSP (HTIF tohost); timed region measured via mcycle CSR (start_trigger/stop_trigger)

scoring.method: Embench-IoT v1.0 speed score: geometric mean of per-benchmark relative speed
scoring.relative_speed_formula: reference_cycles / measured_timed_cycles, where reference_cycles = baseline_ms * 1000 (STM32F4 Cortex-M4 @ 16 MHz, normalised to CPU_MHZ=1 iteration count)
scoring.interpretation: score of 1.0 == cycle-for-cycle parity with the Cortex-M4 reference; >1 == fewer cycles per benchmark than the reference
scoring.caveat: Reported geometric_mean_score is over all 16 scored workloads and includes two scale-distorted outliers (nbody, st) whose timed regions execute too few iterations at CPU_MHZ=1 to be steady-state-meaningful. Prefer geometric_mean_score_excluding_scale_outliers (14 workloads) for IPC-class comparison. All cycle counts are exact mcycle deltas from the cycle-accurate CVA6 RTL model.

coverage.note: Embench v1.0 ships 19 speed workloads; the manifest also lists primecount and tarfind which were introduced in Embench 2.0 and are absent at the pinned v1.0 tag.

statemate.reason: Did not reach a clean tohost completion within a bounded wall-clock budget on the CVA6 veri-testharness model. statemate is by far the heaviest Embench v1.0 workload on this cycle-accurate RTL model; repeated isolated single-process runs exceeded ~28 min of 100%-CPU simulation without reaching the stop_trigger/tohost write. No FAILED assertion and no AXI decode/slave error were observed (3 benign B-response warnings during early M-mode boot only), i.e. this is a simulation-throughput limit on the directed cycle-accurate model, not a functional/ISA defect.
statemate.next_command: E1_EMBENCH_SKIP_BUILD=1 nohup build/embench/statemate.elf run with an extended budget, or lower the workload scale (WARMUP_HEAT/LOCAL_SCALE_FACTOR) for statemate only, then re-collect with E1_EMBENCH_SKIP_BUILD=1 E1_EMBENCH_SKIP_RUN=1 make embench

huffbench.reason: Never reaches main()/the timed region: sustained AXI write-response errors (ariane_testharness.sv:676 'B Response Errored', RESP_DECERR/SLVERR) begin at ~cycle 56k during bare-metal startup and continue to the harness time_out; no EMBENCH_CYCLES and no clean SUCCESS are emitted. The 16 scored workloads (built from the identical crt.S/syscalls.c BSP and link.ld) show zero such errors, so this is a workload-specific interaction between the huffbench bare-metal port and the testharness AXI memory decode, not a global BSP defect.
huffbench.next_command: Re-link huffbench against the riscv-tests p/v environment (or relocate its .bss heap array out of the tohost-adjacent page) and re-run; inspect build/embench/huffbench.run.log for the first faulting address.

qrduino.reason: Identical failure signature to huffbench: sustained AXI 'B Response Errored' (RESP_DECERR/SLVERR) from ~cycle 63k in startup, never reaches main(), no EMBENCH_CYCLES, no SUCCESS. Workload-specific (qrduino + testharness AXI decode), reproducible and load-independent.
qrduino.next_command: Same remediation as huffbench: re-link against riscv-tests env / relocate the static heap array, then re-run.

scale_outliers.explanation: nbody and st run only a few thousand timed cycles at CPU_MHZ=1 (LOCAL_SCALE_FACTOR yields very few benchmark iterations), so their reference/measured ratio is dominated by fixed startup and is not a meaningful steady-state IPC comparison. They inflate the all-scored geometric mean. The excluding-scale-outliers geomean over the remaining 14 workloads is the more representative figure.
